images.caption: Figure 1:Inference procedure with History Reference.All states are embedded, and the historical states are further processed by HER. The embedding of current stepx~(t)\tilde{x}^{(t)}is added to the history reference (HR) and fed into the model as the input, which predicts the next stepx~(t+1)\tilde{x}^{(t+1)}.

Figure 2:Synthetic history data construction for Mask Diffusion Model Training.
A noisy sequence is created from a clean sequence using mask and wrong-token corruption. Position-wise transition rules are then used to sample synthetic histories and define training targets.

Figure 3:Qualitative results on the image editing task.The masks predicted by RM are highlighted in red. Heat maps at the bottom visualize pixel-wise differences between the edited images and the originals.
Please refer to AppendixDfor the complete editing prompts and more qualitative results.

Figure 4:RM actively re-masks and corrects tokens during inference based on the evolving global context.
As the preceding context is refined, the correction propagates to the final answer. Please refer to AppendixEfor more results and representative revision trajectories.
